definition: A set of nodes and edges used to represent computational relationships.
An entity state object document task concept or component in a graph.
A connection relation transition dependency similarity flow or reference between nodes.
A graph whose edges have source and target orientation.
A graph whose edges represent symmetric connection.
A graph whose edges carry values such as cost distance time confidence or capacity.
A mapping from each node to its neighboring nodes.
A matrix recording whether or how nodes connect.
A list of source-target relationship records.
A path that returns to its start.
Whether one node can be reached from another.
A connected region of a graph.
A measure of node importance under a specific definition.
A directed graph representing prerequisites or dependencies.
A graph representing flow through capacity-constrained edges.
A graph of typed entities and relationships.
A representation risk where an edge or path implies more than evidence supports.
